summary: Marcus, Spielman, and Srivastava (2015) resolved the conjecture for bipartite $d$-regular graphs: using the method of interlacing polynomials, they proved that every bipartite $d$-regular graph has a 2-lift with all new eigenvalues of magnitude at most $2\sqrt{d-1}$, yielding infinite families of bipartite Ramanujan graphs of every degree $> 2$. Hall, Puder, and Sawin (2018) further extended this to $r$-sheeted coverings for bipartite Ramanujan graphs. However, the original Bilu–Linial conjecture for general (non-bipartite) $d$-regular graphs remains open.

since_posted.title: Interlacing families I: Bipartite Ramanujan graphs of all degrees
since_posted.authors: Adam W. Marcus, Daniel A. Spielman, Nikhil Srivastava
since_posted.claim: Proves the bipartite case of the Bilu–Linial conjecture: every bipartite $d$-regular graph has a 2-lift with all new eigenvalues of magnitude at most $2\sqrt{d-1}$, establishing bipartite Ramanujan graphs of every degree via a new method of interlacing polynomials.

since_posted.title: Ramanujan Coverings of Graphs
since_posted.authors: Chris Hall, Doron Puder, William F. Sawin
since_posted.claim: Generalises the MSS bipartite 2-lift result to $r$-sheeted coverings: for every $r \geq 2$ and every bipartite Ramanujan graph $G$, there exists an $r$-covering of $G$ in which all new eigenvalues are bounded by the spectral radius of the universal cover.

notes: The MSS paper (arXiv April 2013) resolves only the bipartite case of the Bilu–Linial conjecture; the conjecture for general non-bipartite d-regular graphs appears to remain open as of the latest searches. The Hall–Puder–Sawin paper (arXiv June 2015, Advances in Mathematics 2018) extends to r-coverings but still works within the bipartite framework. No post-2018 resolution of the non-bipartite case was found in up to 5 queries.
